Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: * Input: A textual approximation of a Pseudo-BPMN process representing a multinational smartphone supply chain (from market research to sales cycle monitoring). * Task: Generate 20 open-ended, thought-provoking questions based on this process. * Topics: Rationale behind process flows, potential improvements, risk management, decision-making, stakeholder communication, and performance measurement. * Constraint: Do NOT provide any SQL queries. Just list the questions.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Process Flow: * Start: Market Research -> R&D/Prototype. * Procurement: Parallel sourcing of components (Microprocessors, Cameras, Screens, Sensors) from specific regions (Taiwan, Japan, Korea, Germany/USA). * Logistics: Ship to Malaysia Assembly. * Manufacturing: 24/7 Assembly -> Quality Check (Exclusive Gateway: Pass/Fail). * Packaging: Pack & Label. * Distribution: Split into Air Freight (High Demand) vs. Cargo Ship (Other). * Compliance: Customs/Regulatory. * Receiving: Regional Distribution Centers (Europe, NA, Asia, LatAm). * Last Mile: Split into Warehousing, Retail, E-Commerce, VIP/Pre-Orders. * Monitoring: Inventory/Sales Forecast -> Decision (Adjust Orders or Proceed). * End: Next Sales Cycle.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Brainstorming Categories for Questions: * Strategic Rationale: Why this flow? Why these regions? Why Malaysia? * Risk Management: Supply chain disruptions, geopolitical issues, quality failures. * Process Improvement: Bottlenecks, parallelization, automation. * Decision Making: The Exclusive Gateways (Quality, Adjust Orders). * Stakeholder Communication: Suppliers, Customers, Internal Teams. * Performance Measurement: KPIs for each stage. * Sustainability/Ethics: Sourcing, Carbon footprint.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 4. Drafting Questions (Iterative Process): * Idea 1 (Sourcing): Why specific countries for components? -> How does geopolitical stability influence the selection of specific component suppliers in Taiwan, Japan, and South Korea? ... * Idea 20 (End Game): Cycle. -> How is the Next Sales Cycle defined, and what triggers the transition from the current End Event to the next Start Event?

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5. Review and Refine: * Check against constraints: 20 questions? Yes. Open-ended? Yes. Thought-provoking? Yes. No SQL? Yes. * Ensure variety in topics (Risk, Process, Strategy, etc.). * Refine wording to be more professional and probing. *Refining Q1:* Instead of Why specific countries, ask about geopolitical risk mitigation. *Refining Q4:* Focus on the loop efficiency.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 6. Final Polish: Ensure no markdown code blocks for the questions list itself (just a list), but the prompt asked to list the questions. I will present them as a numbered list. ... Example: How does the decision to split component procurement into parallel tasks impact the overall coordination overhead compared to the time saved? -> Good.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. Final Check: No SQL. Just questions. 20 questions. Open-ended. Thought-provoking. Ready to generate output.
